stories.title: GitHub CI: comprehensive PR validation (HA gate, lint, vendor audit, bootstrap check)
stories.description:
Create the GitHub Actions CI pipeline that validates every PR to the sovereign repo. This must land at priority 8 — before any further chart work — so all subsequent stories benefit from automated validation. Two workflows:

.github/workflows/validate.yml — runs on every PR and push to main. Five parallel jobs:

Job 1 helm-validate (matrix over every chart in charts/):
helm lint charts/<name>/; helm template charts/<name>/ | kubectl apply --dry-run=client; assert rendered output contains PodDisruptionBudget (fail if missing); assert rendered output contains podAntiAffinity in every Deployment/StatefulSet (fail if missing); assert values.yaml replicaCount >= 2 (fail if 1 or absent); assert no image tag ':latest' anywhere in values.yaml; assert image.repository references global.imageRegistry (not a hardcoded registry).

Job 2 shell-validate: shellcheck on all bootstrap/**/*.sh vendor/*.sh vendor/recipes/*/build.sh; assert every vendor/*.sh handles --dry-run (grep DRY_RUN or dry.run); assert every vendor/*.sh handles --backup.

Job 3 vendor-audit (only runs if vendor/ files changed): run vendor/audit.sh — exits non-zero on license violations; validate VENDORS.yaml schema (every entry has all required fields); validate every recipe.yaml has rollout.strategy, rollout.max_unavailable, backup.priority.

Job 4 argocd-validate: kubectl apply --dry-run=client on all argocd-apps/**/*.yaml; assert every Application has revisionHistoryLimit: 3.

Job 5 bootstrap-validate: shellcheck bootstrap/providers/*.sh; grep bootstrap/bootstrap.sh for node count guard (nodes.count < 3 check must be present); grep each provider script to assert it contains a loop over node count; grep to assert no provider script references a single hard-coded server IP.

.github/workflows/release.yml — on tag push vX.Y.Z: run validate.yml first; package all Helm charts with helm package; publish to GitHub Pages as a Helm repository with helm repo index; create GitHub Release with auto-generated changelog. CI must complete under 5 minutes. Cache helm/kubectl/shellcheck binaries between runs.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/validate.yml triggers on PR and push to main with 5 parallel jobs
helm-validate fails if any chart is missing PodDisruptionBudget in rendered output
helm-validate fails if any chart has replicaCount default < 2
helm-validate fails if any values.yaml contains :latest image tag
helm-validate fails if image.repository does not use global.imageRegistry
shell-validate runs shellcheck on all .sh files and fails on any error
shell-validate fails if any vendor/*.sh lacks --dry-run and --backup handling
vendor-audit runs vendor/audit.sh and fails on license violations
vendor-audit validates recipe.yaml rollout and backup sections are present
argocd-validate dry-runs all ArgoCD Application manifests
argocd-validate fails if any Application lacks revisionHistoryLimit: 3
bootstrap-validate fails if node count guard is absent from bootstrap.sh
.github/workflows/release.yml publishes Helm charts to GitHub Pages on tag
Full CI run completes in under 5 minutes
Each job fails independently with a clear, actionable error message
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Not yet SMART-scored. Run smart-check ceremony before pulling into a sprint.

stories.title: Bootstrap: front door provider interface and VPS hardening framework
stories.description: Create a pluggable 'front door' security provider pattern for the bootstrap. Add bootstrap/frontdoor/interface.sh defining 5 shell function hooks every provider must implement: frontdoor_provision (local: create tunnel/service), frontdoor_install_agent (remote: install daemon on VPS), frontdoor_allowed_cidrs (returns newline-separated CIDRs to whitelist — everything else dropped), frontdoor_configure_dns (point DNS records), frontdoor_connection_info (print how to connect after bootstrap). Add bootstrap/frontdoor/none.sh as the baseline impl (allowed_cidrs prompts for caller IP, no agent, prints SSH instructions). Add hardening scripts that ALWAYS run regardless of front door: bootstrap/hardening/base.sh (unattended-upgrades, fail2ban, auditd, purge unused packages), bootstrap/hardening/ssh.sh (PasswordAuthentication no, PermitRootLogin prohibit-password, PubkeyAuthentication yes), bootstrap/hardening/kernel.sh (CIS benchmark sysctl: tcp_syncookies, rp_filter, dmesg_restrict, suid_dumpable=0), bootstrap/hardening/firewall.sh (UFW default-deny, reads frontdoor_allowed_cidrs output, opens only those CIDRs). Update bootstrap/config.yaml.example to add frontDoor field (default: cloudflare). Update bootstrap/bootstrap.sh to source the chosen front door impl and call hooks in order: harden → frontdoor_provision → frontdoor_install_agent (remote) → frontdoor_configure_dns → firewall.sh → frontdoor_connection_info.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/frontdoor/interface.sh documents all 5 required hooks with usage comments
bootstrap/frontdoor/none.sh implements all 5 hooks (allowed_cidrs prompts for caller IP)
bootstrap/hardening/base.sh installs and enables unattended-upgrades, fail2ban, auditd
bootstrap/hardening/ssh.sh disables password auth, root password login, enforces pubkey
bootstrap/hardening/kernel.sh applies CIS benchmark sysctl settings
bootstrap/hardening/firewall.sh calls frontdoor_allowed_cidrs and applies UFW rules
bootstrap/bootstrap.sh sources frontdoor impl and calls hooks in correct order
bootstrap/config.yaml.example has frontDoor field with cloudflare as default
shellcheck passes on all new .sh files
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Not yet SMART-scored. Run smart-check ceremony before pulling into a sprint.

stories.title: Bootstrap: Cloudflare front door implementation
stories.description: Implement bootstrap/frontdoor/cloudflare.sh with all 5 interface hooks using Cloudflare Tunnel + Zero Trust Access (free tier). frontdoor_provision: use Cloudflare API (token + account ID from config.yaml) to create a named tunnel, store tunnel credentials JSON, create a cloudflared config.yml for the VPS. frontdoor_install_agent: SSH to VPS, install cloudflared deb package, write /etc/cloudflared/config.yml, enable and start cloudflared.service as a systemd unit. frontdoor_allowed_cidrs: fetch https://www.cloudflare.com/ips-v4 and https://www.cloudflare.com/ips-v6 and echo them (no local ports needed — tunnel is outbound-only). frontdoor_configure_dns: use Cloudflare API to create CNAME records routing *.<domain> to the tunnel hostname. frontdoor_connection_info: print instructions for using 'cloudflare access ssh' and the Zero Trust dashboard URL. Add bootstrap/providers/cloudflare-dns.sh as a shared helper for Cloudflare API calls (zone lookup, record create/update) reusable by both the front door and any provider that needs DNS. config.yaml.example cloudflare section needs: apiToken, accountId, tunnelName, zoneId.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/frontdoor/cloudflare.sh implements all 5 hooks
frontdoor_provision creates a Cloudflare Tunnel via API and writes tunnel credentials
frontdoor_install_agent installs cloudflared on the VPS as a systemd service via SSH
frontdoor_allowed_cidrs fetches live Cloudflare IP ranges (v4 + v6)
frontdoor_configure_dns creates wildcard CNAME via Cloudflare API
bootstrap/providers/cloudflare-dns.sh has reusable API helper functions
config.yaml.example has cloudflare.apiToken, accountId, tunnelName, zoneId
shellcheck passes on all new .sh files
After running: VPS has no open inbound ports, all traffic flows through tunnel
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Not yet SMART-scored. Run smart-check ceremony before pulling into a sprint.

stories.title: Bootstrap HA: multi-node provisioning, kube-vip, and etcd quorum enforcement
stories.description: Refactor all provider scripts and bootstrap.sh to enforce HA as a hard requirement. bootstrap.sh must validate nodes.count is odd and >= 3 — print a clear error and abort if not (single-node and even-node setups are not supported). Update bootstrap/config.yaml.example to add nodes section with count: 3 and serverType. Refactor all four provider scripts (hetzner.sh, generic-vps.sh, aws-ec2.sh, digitalocean.sh) to loop over node count and provision all N nodes. On each provider: after all nodes are running, install kube-vip on each control plane node to create a floating API server VIP. Install K3s using --cluster-init with embedded etcd on node 1 (pointing tls-san at the VIP). Join nodes 2+ using --server https://<VIP>:6443. Wait for all N nodes to be Ready before proceeding. Output kubeconfig pointing at the VIP, not a single node IP. Update bootstrap/verify.sh to check: node count >= 3, all nodes Ready, etcd cluster healthy (etcdctl endpoint health), kube-vip VIP is reachable. Update frontdoor_install_agent hook in bootstrap/frontdoor/interface.sh to accept a list of all node IPs (not just one) — cloudflared must be installed on every node so the tunnel remains up if a node fails. Update cloudflare.sh to install cloudflared on all nodes and create a DaemonSet manifest instead of a single systemd unit. Update all provider cost documentation to show 3-node costs, not single-node. Remove all references to AWS free tier (t2.micro is not viable — minimum is t3.small).
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap.sh aborts with clear error if nodes.count < 3 or even
bootstrap/config.yaml.example has nodes.count: 3 and nodes.serverType
hetzner.sh provisions nodes.count servers in a loop
generic-vps.sh accepts comma-separated list of node IPs for multi-node setup
aws-ec2.sh provisions nodes.count t3.small (minimum) instances
digitalocean.sh provisions nodes.count droplets
kube-vip installed on all control plane nodes before K3s joins
K3s node 1 uses --cluster-init, nodes 2+ use --server https://<VIP>:6443
bootstrap/verify.sh checks all nodes Ready, etcd healthy, VIP reachable
frontdoor_install_agent receives all node IPs and installs cloudflared on each
cloudflare.sh deploys cloudflared as a K3s manifest (DaemonSet) not just systemd
Provider docs updated: all costs show 3-node totals, AWS free tier removed
shellcheck passes on all modified scripts
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

stories.title: Vendor manifest: VENDORS.yaml schema, license audit, and initial inventory
stories.description: Create the vendor manifest system — the 'ebuild database' of the platform. Create vendor/VENDORS.yaml defining every upstream project the platform depends on. Schema per entry: name, upstream_url, license_spdx, license_allows_vendor (bool — false for BSL/SSPL/AGPL), version_pinned, fetch_method (git_tag|tarball), distroless_compatible (yes|partial|no), distroless_base (gcr.io/distroless/static|java17|nodejs|none), deprecated (bool), deprecated_reason, alternative. Populate with ALL services in the current stack: cilium, crossplane, cert-manager, sealed-secrets, vault (flag BSL, alternative: openbao), keycloak, rook-ceph, gitlab, harbor, argocd, istio, opa-gatekeeper, trivy-operator, falco, prometheus-stack, loki, thanos, tempo, backstage, code-server, sonarqube, reportportal, selenium-grid, k6-operator, wiremock, mailhog, chaos-mesh. Create vendor/audit.sh that reads VENDORS.yaml and prints a report: license violations, distroless-incompatible services, deprecated entries with alternatives. Exit non-zero if any entry has license_allows_vendor: false without an alternative specified. NOTE: Vault must be marked license_allows_vendor: false with alternative: openbao. Update prd.json story 008 description to reference OpenBao (openbao/openbao) instead of HashiCorp Vault so the next iteration rebuilds that chart correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: vendor/VENDORS.yaml exists with all 27+ services from the current stack
Every entry has all required schema fields

vendor/audit.sh exits non-zero when any entry lacks an alternative for a non-vendorable license
shellcheck passes on vendor/audit.sh

stories.title: Vendor distroless audit: compatibility matrix, recipe stubs, and platform standard
stories.description: Create the definitive distroless compatibility audit. Create vendor/DISTROLESS.md as a living document with a table per vendor: service, language/runtime, distroless base image, status (compatible/partial/incompatible/rewrite-candidate), notes. Go-based services (cilium, cert-manager, crossplane, argocd, sealed-secrets, harbor, trivy-operator, openbao, thanos, loki, tempo, k6-operator) → distroless/static, mark compatible. Java-based (keycloak) → distroless/java21, mark partial with note suggesting Kanidm (Rust, native distroless) as long-term alternative. Node-based (backstage, code-server) → distroless/nodejs, mark partial. Shell-script containers → incompatible, note they run outside cluster. Create vendor/recipes/ directory with a recipe.yaml stub for each service — schema: name, upstream_url, version, license_spdx, fetch_method, distroless_base, build_tool (ko|docker|skip). Update CLAUDE.md to add a DISTROLESS STANDARD section: all new container images must use distroless base; PRs adding non-distroless images require explicit deprecated entry in VENDORS.yaml with migration plan.
acceptanceCriteria: vendor/DISTROLESS.md has a row for every service in VENDORS.yaml
All Go-based services marked as distroless/static compatible
Keycloak marked partial with Kanidm noted as Rust alternative
vendor/recipes/ directory has recipe.yaml stub for every service
recipe.yaml schema has: name, upstream_url, version, license_spdx, fetch_method, distroless_base, build_tool
CLAUDE.md updated with DISTROLESS STANDARD section

stories.title: Vendor fetch framework: Gentoo-style source fetching into internal GitLab
stories.description: Build the source fetching framework. Equivalent to Gentoo portage fetch — gets upstream source into internal GitLab without creating runtime dependency on external git hosts. Create vendor/fetch.sh [name|all]: for each recipe in vendor/recipes/<name>/recipe.yaml, clone or update the upstream repo into the internal GitLab at gitlab.<domain>/vendor/<name> using GitLab API (GITLAB_TOKEN from env). Create the GitLab group 'vendor' and project if they don't exist, then mirror the upstream at the pinned version. Create vendor/update-check.sh: queries each upstream for new releases (GitHub API or git ls-remote), compares to pinned version in recipe.yaml, outputs report of available updates, optionally creates GitLab issues. Create vendor/pin.sh <name> <version>: updates version in recipe.yaml. Fetch method implementations: git_tag (clone at tag), tarball (download release tarball, verify SHA256 from upstream checksums file), git_commit (pin to SHA). Populate vendor/recipes/ with full recipe.yaml for the 10 most fundamental services: cilium, cert-manager, crossplane, argocd, sealed-secrets, openbao, keycloak, harbor, rook-ceph, prometheus-stack. All scripts must work with the GitLab instance running from story 014.
acceptanceCriteria: vendor/fetch.sh fetches a single named recipe or all recipes
vendor/fetch.sh creates GitLab group 'vendor' and project if not exists via API
vendor/fetch.sh mirrors upstream at pinned version into internal GitLab
vendor/update-check.sh reports available updates vs pinned versions
vendor/pin.sh <name> <version> updates recipe.yaml
vendor/recipes/ has full recipe.yaml for 10 fundamental services
vendor/fetch.sh --dry-run prints actions without making API calls
shellcheck passes on all vendor/*.sh
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Not yet SMART-scored. Run smart-check ceremony before pulling into a sprint.

stories.title: Vendor build recipes: ko for Go, multi-stage Docker, patch application, SHA verification
stories.description: Implement the build recipe system — each service has a build.sh that speaks the upstream's own build toolchain, applies patches, and produces a distroless OCI image. No Bazel. Reproducibility comes from: pinned git SHA in recipe.yaml + upstream go.sum/Cargo.lock (upstream's own lock file) + pinned compiler version in recipe.yaml. vendor/fetch.sh SHA verification: after cloning at the pinned tag, assert `git rev-parse HEAD == recipe.git_sha` — abort if mismatch (tag was tampered with). Tag state before overwriting: `git tag sovereign/pre-update-<timestamp>` on the internal GitLab mirror. Add git_sha field to recipe.yaml (alongside version tag). For Go services: build.sh uses `ko build` (https://ko.build) — single command, automatically produces distroless images, uses the project's own go.mod/go.sum, pinned Go version from recipe.yaml go_version field. For Java services (keycloak): multi-stage Dockerfile — builder stage uses gradle/maven from source, final stage copies artifact into gcr.io/distroless/java21. For Node.js services (backstage): multi-stage Dockerfile — builder with npm ci, final stage uses gcr.io/distroless/nodejs. vendor/build.sh [name|all]: checks out source from internal GitLab at pinned SHA, applies vendor/recipes/<name>/patches/*.patch in alphabetical order, calls vendor/recipes/<name>/build.sh, tags image as <version>-<source_sha>-p<patch_count>, pushes to harbor.<domain>/sovereign-staging/<name> (not production — deploy.sh promotes). Bakes OCI labels: org.sovereign.source_sha, org.sovereign.patches_applied, org.sovereign.compiler_version, org.sovereign.build_timestamp. vendor/verify-distroless.sh: runs each built image with the equivalent of --version, then attempts `docker exec <container> /bin/sh` and asserts it fails with 'not found'. Implement build.sh for: cert-manager (Go/ko), crossplane (Go/ko), argocd (Go/ko), sealed-secrets (Go/ko), openbao (Go/ko).
acceptanceCriteria: recipe.yaml has git_sha field alongside version tag
vendor/fetch.sh verifies git rev-parse HEAD == recipe.git_sha after clone, aborts if mismatch
vendor/fetch.sh tags pre-update state before overwriting mirror
vendor/build.sh applies patches in alphabetical order before building
vendor/build.sh tags images as <version>-<source_sha>-p<patch_count>
vendor/build.sh pushes to sovereign-staging (not production)
Built images have OCI labels for source_sha, patches_applied, compiler_version, build_timestamp
vendor/recipes/cert-manager/build.sh uses ko build
vendor/recipes/crossplane/build.sh uses ko build
vendor/recipes/argocd/build.sh uses ko build
vendor/recipes/sealed-secrets/build.sh uses ko build
vendor/recipes/openbao/build.sh uses ko build
vendor/verify-distroless.sh confirms no shell in built images
shellcheck passes on all vendor/*.sh and vendor/recipes/*/build.sh

stories.title: Vendor pipeline: GitLab CI triggers, ArgoCD Image Updater, Harbor-first Helm values
stories.description: Wire up the full vendor pipeline: source in GitLab → ko/docker build in GitLab CI → push to Harbor → ArgoCD Image Updater detects new tag → ArgoCD syncs updated Helm release. Install ArgoCD Image Updater as an ArgoCD Application (argocd-apps/platform/argocd-image-updater-app.yaml) watching harbor.<domain>/sovereign/*. Create vendor/gitlab-ci-template.yml: on new tag pushed to internal mirror → ko/docker build → push harbor.<domain>/sovereign/<name>:<version> → create GitLab release. Update charts/_globals/values.yaml to add global.imageRegistry defaulting to harbor.<domain>/sovereign (empty string for bootstrap mode before Harbor is up). Update every chart's values.yaml image.repository to use global.imageRegistry prefix. Add ArgoCD Image Updater annotations to every ArgoCD Application manifest. Create vendor/smoke-test.sh: pulls each sovereign/* image from Harbor, runs --version, verifies image starts without a shell (distroless validation). This story completes the autarky loop: zero external image pulls after bootstrap.
acceptanceCriteria: ArgoCD Image Updater installed as ArgoCD Application with Harbor credentials
vendor/gitlab-ci-template.yml defines fetch→build→push pipeline
charts/_globals/values.yaml has global.imageRegistry

vendor/smoke-test.sh validates each sovereign/* image is distroless
helm lint passes on all updated charts

Command: grep -n 'replicaCount' charts/sonarqube/values.yaml — Output: '33: replicaCount: 1' (nested under sonarqube: subchart key). helm template charts/sonarqube/ | grep 'replicas:' — Output: 'replicas: 1' (SonarQube pod). SonarQube Community Edition supports only 1 replica. Fix: add ha_exception: true entry in vendor/VENDORS.yaml for sonarqube with ha_exception_reason, AND add top-level replicaCount: 1 with a comment referencing the VENDORS.yaml entry. reportportal chart: replicaCount: 2 — PASS. All other ACs pass.

Suggested testPlan: for chart in selenium-grid k6-operator wiremock mailhog chaos-mesh; do helm lint charts/$chart/; done; for chart in selenium-grid k6-operator wiremock mailhog chaos-mesh; do helm template charts/$chart/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget || echo MISSING in $chart; done; helm template charts/selenium-grid/ | grep -i 'host:'; kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f argocd-apps/testing/.

stories.title: HA pass: PodDisruptionBudgets, anti-affinity, and replica hardening for all charts
stories.description: Audit every Helm chart in charts/ and ensure HA is properly configured. This story is a systematic pass over all charts to enforce the HA standards documented in CLAUDE.md. For each chart: (1) ensure default replicaCount >= 2 in values.yaml (3 for critical: argocd, keycloak, openbao, gitlab, harbor); (2) add PodDisruptionBudget template with minAvailable: 1; (3) add podAntiAffinity — preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution for standard services, requiredDuring for critical services to guarantee cross-node spread; (4) verify readinessProbe and livenessProbe are defined for every container; (5) verify resources.requests and resources.limits are set. For StatefulSets (gitlab, keycloak, harbor, openbao, rook-ceph): ensure volumeClaimTemplates use global.storageClass. For DaemonSets (cilium, cloudflared): skip replica count (N/A) but verify resource limits. Create charts/_globals/templates/_ha-helpers.tpl with shared PDB and anti-affinity template helpers to avoid duplication. Update ArgoCD Application manifests to set revisionHistoryLimit: 3 (keep last 3 deployable revisions for rollback). Run helm lint and helm template dry-run on every chart after changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Every chart has replicaCount >= 2 as default (>= 3 for argocd, keycloak, openbao, gitlab, harbor)

charts/_globals/templates/_ha-helpers.tpl has shared PDB and anti-affinity helpers

stories.title: GitLab CI: vendor build pipeline, SAST/SCA scanning, and CVE issue creation
stories.description:
Create the GitLab CI pipeline template and runners inside the cluster for building vendor images and continuous security scanning. This is entirely separate from GitHub CI: GitHub validates the sovereign repo structure, GitLab builds images from vendored source and runs security tools against the full source tree.

vendor/gitlab-ci-template.yml — included by every mirrored repo at gitlab.<domain>/vendor/<name>. Stages: verify, patch, build, scan-sast, scan-sca, scan-license, push, notify.

verify: assert `git rev-parse HEAD` matches recipe.yaml git_sha — abort with clear error if mismatch (tag was tampered with or recipe.yaml is stale).
patch: apply vendor/recipes/<name>/patches/*.patch in alphabetical order.
build: call vendor/recipes/<name>/build.sh → produces OCI image tagged <version>-<source_sha>-p<patch_count>. Uses kaniko (not docker-in-docker).
scan-sast: run Semgrep with semgrep/semgrep-rules:auto against patched source tree. Findings written to semgrep-results.json and uploaded as GitLab artifact.
scan-sca: run Trivy fs . against source to find vulnerable dependencies in go.sum/package-lock.json/Cargo.lock/pom.xml. Critical findings block push stage.
scan-license: run license-checker against all dependency manifests. Fail if any transitive dependency has BSL/SSPL/proprietary license that snuck in.
push: only runs if all scan stages pass. Push to harbor.<domain>/sovereign-staging/<name>:<tag>.
notify: for each critical CVE in scan-sca output, create a GitLab issue in the vendor/<name> project with: CVE ID, CVSS score, affected package, fixed version (if available), NVD link, and a suggested patch approach. Deduplicate: skip if issue with same CVE ID already open.

GitLab Runner: install as ArgoCD Application using the gitlab/gitlab-runner Helm chart. Kubernetes executor. Runner pods: restricted PSA, no privileged, resource limits. Use kaniko executor image for Docker builds.

Scheduled pipeline: vendor/fetch.sh --all + full scan runs weekly via GitLab CI schedule to catch newly disclosed CVEs against pinned-version source even without code changes. Weekly scan results dashboard visible in GitLab Security Dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: vendor/gitlab-ci-template.yml defines all 8 stages
verify stage aborts pipeline if HEAD SHA does not match recipe.yaml git_sha
build stage uses kaniko (no privileged containers, no docker-in-docker)
scan-sca creates GitLab issue for each critical CVE with CVE ID, score, NVD link
scan-sca deduplicates: does not create duplicate issues for already-open CVEs
scan-license fails pipeline if transitive dep has BSL/SSPL/proprietary license
push stage only runs when all scan stages pass
GitLab Runner installed as ArgoCD Application with Kubernetes executor
Runner pods run under restricted PodSecurityAdmission with resource limits
argocd-apps/platform/gitlab-runner-app.yaml exists and passes kubectl dry-run
Weekly scheduled pipeline configured for fetch+scan across all vendored repos
helm lint passes on GitLab Runner ArgoCD Application
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Not yet SMART-scored. Run smart-check ceremony before pulling into a sprint.

stories.readinessNote: ACs #4 ('scan-sca creates GitLab issue for each critical CVE'), #5 ('scan-sca deduplicates'), and #11 ('Weekly scheduled pipeline configured') require a running GitLab instance and cannot be verified statically. These should be noted as live-test requirements in the blocker protocol. testPlan is generic — should specify the statically-verifiable subset: cat vendor/gitlab-ci-template.yml | yq e '.' -; grep -c 'stage:' vendor/gitlab-ci-template.yml; grep kaniko vendor/gitlab-ci-template.yml; helm lint charts/gitlab-runner/; kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f argocd-apps/platform/gitlab-runner-app.yaml.

stories.title: Planning ceremony: enforce SMART achievable threshold before sprint pull
stories.description: The planning ceremony must reject any story with achievable score < 3 from being pulled into a sprint without first being split. Story 026 entered sprint 6 with achievable=2 and SMART notes saying 'NOT READY — split into 026a/b/c', yet was pulled anyway. Add an explicit check: if achievable < 3, block the pull and require the story to be split or re-scored with justification.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/plan.py (or plan.md) rejects stories where smart.achievable < 3
Rejection message names the story ID, current achievable score, and instructs operator to split or rescore
Test: a mock sprint_candidates list with achievable=2 story triggers the gate and exits non-zero
Gate can be overridden with --force-achievable flag with a required reason string (audit trail)
stories.testPlan: Run the planning ceremony against a fixture with an achievable=2 story; verify it exits non-zero and prints the correct message.
stories.returnedReason: Root cause: planning ceremony pulled story 026 (achievable=2, NOT READY) into sprint 6 without enforcing split requirement.

smart.notes: Specific: gate logic + override flag fully described. Measurable: exit code + message are binary. Achievable: small script change. Relevant: prevents repeat of sprint 6 waste. TimeBound: bounded by the test fixture criterion.

stories.title: GitHub Actions CI: lint + shellcheck + yq on every PR
stories.description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the same static gates as the smoke test ceremony on every pull request. No commit should be able to merge with a broken chart, invalid shell script, or malformed ArgoCD manifest. The gate must run without a cluster — pure static analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yaml exists and triggers on pull_request to main
Workflow runs helm lint on every chart in charts/
Workflow runs shellcheck on every .sh file in the repo
Workflow runs yq e '.' on every yaml file in argocd-apps/
A PR with a deliberate helm lint error fails the workflow
A PR with a shellcheck error fails the workflow
Workflow completes in under 3 minutes on a cold runner
stories.testPlan: Open a test PR with a deliberate lint error, verify workflow fails. Fix it, verify workflow passes.

stories.title: Real smoke test: helm install --dry-run against kind cluster
stories.description: The current smoke test is static analysis only — it checks chart syntax but not deployability. Add a ceremony gate that stands up a kind cluster, runs helm install --dry-run for every chart, and tears the cluster down. A chart that passes lint but fails install is a real defect. This gate catches it.
acceptanceCriteria: kind/setup.sh creates a single-node kind cluster with the name 'sovereign-smoke'
scripts/ralph/lib/gates.py smoke_test() invokes kind install-dry-run after static checks
helm install --dry-run --generate-name runs for every chart in charts/ against the kind cluster
Any chart that fails helm install --dry-run is reported as a smoke failure with full output
kind cluster is torn down after the smoke test regardless of pass/fail
The gate completes in under 5 minutes
Existing static analysis checks (helm lint, shellcheck, yq) are preserved and run first
stories.testPlan: Introduce a chart with a valid helm lint but broken template (e.g. undefined value reference). Verify smoke gate catches it. Verify kind cluster is cleaned up on failure.
stories.readinessNote: depends on story 038 which has passes: false — blocked

stories.title: Migrate 'phase' concept to 'increment' — align vocabulary with product funnel
stories.description: The system has two parallel organizing hierarchies: Themes→Epics→Stories (product funnel) and Phases→Sprints (install-order sequencing). These use different vocabulary for the same execution concept. Phases are named after infrastructure install order ('bootstrap', 'foundations') rather than product capability milestones. Standardize on 'increment' as the execution container, align increment names to theme/epic goals, remove the redundant `phase` int field from stories (epicId already implies the increment), and fix advance.py arithmetic which breaks on non-integer phase IDs like '2h' and '2i'.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json uses 'increments' and 'currentIncrement' instead of 'phases' and 'currentPhase'
Each increment entry has a 'themeGoal' field describing what theme capability it advances
Sprint files renamed from prd/phase-N-*.json to prd/increment-N-*.json
Stories in backlog.json no longer have a standalone 'phase' int field — sequencing expressed via story 'dependencies' only
advance.py handles non-integer increment IDs without arithmetic — uses ordered list position instead
ceremonies.py, orient.py, and all lib/ modules use 'increment' not 'phase' consistently
docs/state/architecture.md vocabulary table updated to reflect the unified model
All existing sprint history data preserved and migrated correctly
stories.testPlan: Run ceremonies.py --dry-run after migration. Verify orient reads increments correctly. Verify advance moves from current to next increment without arithmetic errors.

stories.title: Document and enforce HA exception process in quality gate #12
stories.description: Some upstream services (SonarQube CE, MailHog, single-node Elasticsearch) architecturally cannot scale horizontally. These services fail HA gate #12 (replicaCount >= 2) even when correctly configured. Add a documented exception pathway: (1) Add ha_exception schema fields to vendor/VENDORS.yaml (ha_exception: bool, ha_exception_reason: string, ha_exception_dce_alternative: string). (2) Add entries for SonarQube CE and any other known single-instance upstreams already in the vendor list. (3) Update the quality gate #12 check description in CLAUDE.md to state: 'Exception: if vendor/VENDORS.yaml has ha_exception: true for this service, replicaCount: 1 is acceptable — gate passes only if the exception is documented AND the chart has a top-level replicaCount: 1 with a comment referencing it.'
acceptanceCriteria: vendor/VENDORS.yaml schema includes ha_exception and ha_exception_reason fields
sonarqube entry in vendor/VENDORS.yaml has ha_exception: true with ha_exception_reason explaining CE single-instance architecture
charts/sonarqube/values.yaml has top-level replicaCount: 1 with a comment referencing the VENDORS.yaml exception
helm lint passes on charts/sonarqube/
HA gate #12 documentation in CLAUDE.md updated to reference the exception process (retro-patch-phase7.md contains the proposed wording)
stories.testPlan: grep for ha_exception in VENDORS.yaml, helm lint charts/sonarqube/, verify replicaCount comment exists.
stories.returnedReason: Root cause of story-029 repeated review failure: no documented HA exception process for single-instance upstreams.
